title: dep add: mixed-kind cycle (blocks + parent-child) silently accepted, locks both issues out of ready
body:
# Findings

`jjf dep add` only checks for cycles among `blocks`-kind edges (guard added in `d32ec955`). Mixed-kind cycles ŌĆö specifically `A blocks B` combined with `B parent-of A` ŌĆö are silently accepted. After the cycle is established, the `is_blocked` computation in `list_ready` propagates as follows:

1. A has a `blocks` dep on B ŌåÆ A is blocked (B is open and active).
2. B has a `parent-child` dep on A ŌåÆ B is blocked (A is blocked and active).

Result: both A and B are permanently absent from `jjf ready` with no error or warning. There is no escape except to manually delete one of the edges.

Reproduced with the QA harness (`sub2-wronganswer.sh`, attack B3-i):

```
jjf dep add <A> <B> --kind blocks    # accepted, exit 0
jjf dep add <B> <A> --kind parent-child  # also accepted, exit 0 ŌĆö cycle not detected
jjf ready --json                     # returns empty or omits both A and B
```

Relevant source: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` ŌĆö the cycle preflight at `add_dep_edge` (line ~2389) only fires when `kind == DepKind::Blocks`. The comment at line ~2347 acknowledges: "parent-child cycles are a follow-up (also harmful)."

# Recommendation

Extend the cycle preflight to also detect cross-kind cycles that would create a blocking loop. At minimum: before accepting a `parent-child` edge from child C to parent P, verify that no path from P to C exists in the combined `{blocks, parent-child}` blocking graph. Alternatively, at `list_ready` time, detect cycles in the combined graph and surface a warning rather than silently omitting both issues.

A simpler near-term fix: add an additional check ŌĆö if the new `parent-child` edge from C ŌåÆ P would place C in the `blocked` set (because C already blocks P via `blocks`), reject it as a cycle.

# Confidence

High ŌĆö reproduced deterministically; the code path is clearly missing the cross-kind cycle guard. The existing comment in `lib.rs` (`"parent-child cycles are a follow-up (also harmful)"`) confirms this was known-deferred.

# Open follow-ups

- Decide scope: should the fix cover all cross-kind cycles, or only the mixed `blocks` + `parent-child` case? The `related` and `discovered-from` edge kinds don't participate in `is_blocked`, so they can't create silent lockouts.
- File a follow-up if the fix covers `parent-child`-only cycles too (e.g., A parent-of B + B parent-of A).
